subject: Applied to Employee Experience PM — would love your perspective
body:
Hi Cheney,

I recently applied to the Employee Experience Program Manager role at Axon's Seattle office and wanted to personally reach out.

I'm drawn to the role because of how it sits at the intersection of data, cross-functional collaboration, and building meaningful employee programs — which is exactly how I've approached my work in People Ops and OD. At NextGen Healthcare I analyzed 2,000+ employee survey responses to shape C-suite L&D strategy, and at Vestas I owned an M&A data integration project and a DEI pilot that scaled to 23 global locations.

As a fellow People practitioner, I'd genuinely love to hear what it's like day-to-day on the HRBP side at Axon — what are some of the exciting opportunities and challenges you see on the team?

If you're open to a brief chat, I'd really appreciate it — I have time in the next two weeks if you're free.

Warm regards,
Jamie (Yi-Chieh) Cheng
jamiecheng0103@gmail.com

subject: Applied to Axon's Employee Experience PM role — quick introduction
body:
Hi Katie,

I recently applied to the Employee Experience Program Manager role at Axon's Seattle office and wanted to reach out directly.

The role caught my attention because of its focus on building programs that connect employee recognition, culture, and cross-functional impact — areas where I have hands-on experience. In my current role at InGenius Prep I manage 20+ programs end-to-end across Marketing, Sales, and Tech, and previously at Vestas and NextGen Healthcare I supported engagement strategy and large-scale HR initiatives.

I know your focus at Axon is executive recruiting, but I'd be grateful for any perspective you might be willing to share on the Employee Experience team and what success looks like there.

If you have 15 minutes in the next two weeks, I'd love to connect.

Thank you so much,
Jamie (Yi-Chieh) Cheng
jamiecheng0103@gmail.com

subject: Applied to Stripe's People Ops Strategy PM role — quick hello
body:
Hi Julia,

I recently applied to the Program Manager, People Operations Strategy role at Stripe and wanted to personally reach out after seeing your profile.

Your background in People Strategy and program management resonates a lot with what I do — I'm currently a Program Enablement Manager at InGenius Prep where I run cross-functional programs across stakeholder groups and analyze feedback to continuously improve what we build. Before that I worked in HRBP and OD roles at Vestas and NextGen Healthcare, focusing on employee engagement data and cross-functional process improvement.

The Stripe role draws me in specifically because of its emphasis on building scalable People Ops infrastructure grounded in data and collaboration — which is exactly how I approach this work.

As a fellow People Operations practitioner, I'd love to hear what the day-to-day looks like on your team — the opportunities and the challenges. If you have 15 minutes in the next two weeks, I'd really appreciate it.

Warm regards,
Jamie (Yi-Chieh) Cheng
jamiecheng0103@gmail.com

subject: Applied to Ironclad's People Ops Coordinator role — honored to reach out
body:
Hi Jenna,

I recently applied to the People Operations Coordinator role at Ironclad and wanted to reach out directly — I hope that's okay.

I'm a People and Organizational Development professional with an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology (USC, 3.95 GPA) and about three years of experience across HRBP, OD consulting, and program management. I'm drawn to organizations where People Operations is a true strategic lever — and from what I've read about Ironclad, it sounds like that's very much the case on your team.

I'd be genuinely honored to connect — with no agenda beyond learning about how the People Ops function works at Ironclad, and what the team's biggest opportunities and challenges look like right now.

If you have 15 minutes available in the next two weeks, I'd be so grateful for the conversation.

Thank you for your time,
Jamie (Yi-Chieh) Cheng
jamiecheng0103@gmail.com

subject: Applied to the People Operations Associate role — would love your perspective
body:
Hi Lucia,

I recently applied to the People Operations Associate role at 80,000 Hours and wanted to personally reach out.

I've followed 80,000 Hours' work for a while — the mission to help people find high-impact careers genuinely resonates with me. When I saw the People Operations Associate role, I felt it was a strong fit with my background: I have an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology (USC), and my experience spans People Ops support, OD consulting, HR program management, and stakeholder-focused feedback and data analysis.

As a fellow People and Recruiting practitioner, I'd love to hear what drew you to 80,000 Hours and what you find most meaningful (and most challenging) about the work day-to-day.

If you have 15 minutes in the next two weeks, I'd really appreciate the conversation.

Warm regards,
Jamie (Yi-Chieh) Cheng
jamiecheng0103@gmail.com
